meta.feed_title: Latest World News on Fox News

items.title: Iranian dissidents seize on Trump remarks about armed resistance, fueling revival of Reagan doctrine
items.summary: Trump's comments on arming Iranian protesters reignite debate over whether the U.S. should actively support resistance networks against Tehran's regime.

items.title: What Israel wants from an Iran peace deal: No enrichment, missile limits and strict enforcement
items.summary: Israel wants Iran to stop enrichment, dismantle underground nuclear sites, restrict missiles and cut ties to Hamas and Hezbollah, experts say.

items.title: Trump ‘right to be outraged’ by Europe’s betrayal on Iran, says former Thatcher advisor
items.summary: President Trump is putting Europe on notice, vowing the U.S. will remember the lack of support during the Iran war as Germany, Italy, Spain and others put on warning.

items.title: Argentina investigators zero in on possible origin point of hantavirus in deadly cruise outbreak
items.summary: Argentina officials say a Dutch couple may have contracted hantavirus at a landfill in Ushuaia before boarding a cruise ship that saw three deaths.

items.title: 200,000 small boat arrivals loom amid UK raising threat level to ‘severe’ following recent terror attack
items.summary: The U.K. is approaching 200,000 small boat arrivals across the English Channel since 2018, according to official figures cited by British media reports.

items.title: South African police airlift massive crocodile suspected of eating missing local
items.summary: A massive crocodile suspected of eating a missing businessman was airlifted by helicopter from South Africa's Komati River after being euthanized.

items.title: Three suspected narco-terrorists killed in US military strike on drug-trafficking vessel in Eastern Pacific
items.summary: SOUTHCOM said Tuesday that the U.S. military carried out a lethal strike on a vessel in the Eastern Pacific, killing three suspected narco-terrorists on a drug-trafficking route.

items.title: Zelenskyy blasts ‘absolute cynicism’ as deadly Russian barrage hits Ukraine before planned ceasefire
items.summary: Russian drone and missile strikes killed at least 22 people and wounded more than 80 across Ukraine, as President Volodymyr Zelenskyy blasted Moscow’s “absolute cynicism."

items.title: US-backed pipeline proposal targets global reliance on Strait of Hormuz amid Iran threats
items.summary: The Strait of Hormuz crisis is pushing Gulf states and the U.S. to develop alternative energy routes, reducing dependence on one vulnerable choke point.

items.title: German officials warn Islamist, far-left rhetoric driving spike in antisemitic attacks
items.summary: Germany faces a "new normal" of antisemitism driven by Islamist and left-wing extremist networks, according to a nationwide report by the Central Council of Jews in Germany.

items.title: IDF claims to have taken out Hamas commander who participated in Oct 7
items.summary: The IDF announced it killed Hamas Nukhba Commander Anas Hamed in a targeted Gaza airstrike, saying he raided Israel during the Oct. 7 Nova massacre.

items.title: US kills 2 more suspected drug traffickers in boat strike
items.summary: Joint Task Force Southern Spear conducted a lethal strike on a vessel in the Caribbean tied to suspected drug trafficking, killing two people.

items.title: Alberta separatists say they have enough signatures for referendum on leaving Canada
items.summary: Stay Free Alberta declared it has enough signatures to trigger a vote on Alberta leaving Canada, with a provincewide ballot possible by October 2025.

items.title: China fireworks factory explosion: Video captures devastating aftermath of blast that left at least 26 dead
items.summary: An explosion at a fireworks factory in in China's Hunan province killed at least 26 people and injured 61 others, according to state media reports.

items.title: Major city bans ads for meat, fossil fuels in sweeping crackdown critics call overreach
items.summary: Amsterdam has reportedly banned public ads for meat and fossil fuels, removing burger, airline, and gas-powered car promotions from city billboards.

items.title: Mike Waltz pushes UN resolution to stop Iran mining key global shipping route
items.summary: The U.S. is advancing a UN resolution against Iran's actions in the Strait of Hormuz as military forces secure shipping under Project Freedom.

items.title: Car plows into pedestrian zone killing 2 as police detain driver and investigate motive
items.summary: Two people were killed and several others seriously injured after a car drove into a pedestrian zone in Leipzig, Germany. Police detained a 33-year-old suspect as officials investigate the cause.

items.title: Brief alcohol ban in Damascus sparks concerns about President al-Sharaa's vision for Syria
items.summary: Critics warn new restrictions could signal a shift toward more bans under President Ahmed al-Sharaa given his previous ties to jihadist terror groups.

items.title: Cruise ship outbreak leaves 3 dead as officials delay medical evacuations and probe hantavirus threat
items.summary: A suspected hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship off West Africa has killed three people, with the WHO coordinating emergency medical evacuations.

items.title: Somali pirate and Houthi alliance targets $1T oil trade route with revived hijack tactic
items.summary: Analysts warn of a Red Sea security vacuum as Somali pirates and Houthi-linked groups allegedly collaborate using new tech to hijack oil tankers.

items.title: Cargo ship attacked by small craft near Strait of Hormuz, UK maritime agency says
items.summary: A large cargo ship was attacked near the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday as Iran pushes to assert control over the vital global energy shipping route.

items.title: Examining NATO: Inside the ‘commitment gap’ as US carries alliance deterrence
items.summary: Trump pressures NATO allies to boost defense spending as experts describe a decades-long commitment gap between political promises and military capability.

items.title: 'Killing off the country': Iran executes dozens, arrests 4,000-plus in war crackdown
items.summary: Iran is being accused of intensifying its crackdown on dissent with executions, mass arrests and one of the internet longest shutdowns to stifle protests against the regime.

items.title: Iran’s killer drones increase slaughter in Sudan amid world’s forgotten war
items.summary: Iran is accused of supplying Mohajer-6 attack drones to Sudan's armed forces as the civil war's death toll reportedly reaches as many as 400,000 people.

items.title: Bus plunges into river after trainee driver crash, massive rescue response: reports
items.summary: All four people on board were rescued after a trainee driver sent a bus careening into the River Seine near Paris Thursday, officials said.
